source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

event.top_event: Loss of Load - Dropped Drill Pipe
actions_taken: Medical assistance rendered on rig floor
Injured person evacuated by helicopter to University of Texas Medical Branch Hospital in Galveston, Texas
event.summary: During drill pipe lay-down operations on the Hercules 300 rig, a joint of 5-inch drill pipe fell approximately 25-30 feet after single joint elevators became unlatched when they struck an A-frame bumper at the top of the v-door. The falling pipe struck a Floor Man on the left frontal portion of his hardhat, causing unconsciousness and requiring helicopter evacuation for medical treatment.
key_phrases: safety pin came out from normal position
equipment not maintained per manufacturer specifications
wire used instead of chain to hold pin
Job Safety Analysis failed to identify hazard

threats.name: Safety pin disengagement from single joint elevators

consequences.name: Head injury and loss of consciousness
Requirement for helicopter evacuation and hospitalization

controls.name: Safety pin retention on single joint elevators
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unlatching of elevators upon impact

supporting_text: The safety pin of the single joint elevators came out from it's normal position and did not prevent the latch from coming open when the elevators impacted the A-frame bumper.

Single joint elevators were not maintained as per manufacturer's specifications at the time of the incident. A long piece of wire was being utilized to hold the pin to the body of the single joint elevators instead of the original piece of chain.

controls.name: Equipment maintenance per manufacturer specifications
controls.barrier_role: Maintain integrity of safety pin retention system

supporting_text: Single joint elevators were not maintained as per manufacturer's specifications at the time of the incident.

Lessee failed to maintain equipment as per manufacturer's specifications. Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEM) show a small length of chain attached from the body of the single joint elevators to the pin.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) hazard identification
controls.barrier_role: Identify and mitigate potential hazards in lifting operations

supporting_text: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) failed to identify or mitigate the potential hazard of the pin coming out of the elevators.
JSA failed to identify the hazard of the elevators striking the A-frame bumper or ways to mitigate the drill pipe from striking the A-frame bumper.

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment - Hardhat
controls.barrier_role: Reduce severity of head injury from dropped object impact

supporting_text: the joint of pipe fell approximately 25 to 30 feet striking the Floor Man on the left frontal portion of his hardhat

controls.name: Emergency medical response and evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Provide rapid medical treatment and transport to reduce injury severity

supporting_text: Other drill crew personnel rendered assistance to the IP until Medical help arrived on the rig floor. IP was evacuated by helicopter to University of Texas Medical Branch (UTMB) Hospital in Galveston, Texas, for further evaluation and treatment.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
